summary: Recent updates to the Web Speech functionality in Firefox enhance how the browser manages speech recognition and synthesis tasks. Key improvements include a **better-organized lifecycle for Web Speech**, which streamlines how these tasks are initiated and completed, making them more efficient. Additionally, the browser now supports the ability to **suspend and resume Web Speech when audio focus changes**, ensuring that speech interactions continue smoothly even if other audio elements are active. These changes not only improve the overall performance of Web Speech but also foster a more user-friendly experience when interacting with voice interfaces.
